title: Fish Tacos with Cabbage Slaw
description: These family-friendly fish tacos are quick to prepare and packed with flavor, featuring crispy fish and a zesty cabbage slaw. Perfect for a fun weeknight meal that even the little ones will enjoy!
instructions: Pat fish fillets dry with paper towels. In a small bowl, mix chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Rub the spice mixture evenly over both sides of the fish.
Heat olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Cook fish for 3-4 minutes per side, or until flaky and cooked through. Break into large pieces with a fork.
While fish cooks, thinly slice the cabbage. In a medium bowl, combine mayonnaise and the juice of one lime. Add the shredded cabbage and toss to coat. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Warm tortillas according to package directions (microwave, oven, or skillet).
Assemble tacos: Place fish pieces in the center of each warm tortilla, top with a generous spoonful of cabbage slaw, and a squeeze of fresh lime juice from the remaining lime.
tips: For extra crispiness, you can lightly pan-fry the tortillas before assembling.
If your kids are picky about slaw, you can serve the cabbage and dressing separately.
Add a sprinkle of fresh cilantro or a dash of hot sauce for adult servings.
